remember: Students will be able to recall the plot and characters of the story 'The Third Level'.
Students will be able to remember the themes and symbols used in the story.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of the third level and its significance in the story.
Students will be able to comprehend the narrator's perspective and his experiences in the story.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of escapism from reality as depicted in the story to real-life situations.
Students will be able to use the story as an example to illustrate the human desire to transcend the mundane and ordinary.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the psychological insights into the human mind as presented in the story.
Students will be able to critically evaluate the themes of reality, illusion, and the human condition in the story.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to develop the ability to comprehend and interpret a variety of texts, including fiction and non-fiction, to promote critical thinking and analytical skills.
To understand the literary devices and techniques used by the author to convey the themes and ideas in the story.
board_exam_objectives: What CBSE tests from this chapter is the ability to comprehend and interpret literary texts, to analyze and evaluate themes and characters, and to develop critical thinking and writing skills.
To assess the student's understanding of the story, its themes, and literary devices, and to evaluate their ability to express their ideas and opinions in a clear and coherent manner.
